proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_tamoxifen` have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_tamoxifen`.
Patients receiving `treatment_palbociclib` have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_palbociclib`.
Patients receiving `treatment_trastuzumab` have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_trastuzumab`.
Patients receiving `treatment_olaparib` have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_olaparib`.
Patients receiving `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan`.
Patients receiving `treatment_pembrolizumab` have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_pembrolizumab`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_tamoxifen` had an adjusted coefficient of -0.008 months (SE about 0.011, p=0.4935), so the hypothesized benefit was not supported.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_palbociclib` had a large positive adjusted coefficient of 1.102 months (SE about 0.011, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_trastuzumab` had an adjusted coefficient of -0.010 months (SE about 0.013, p=0.4543), not supporting a main-effect benefit.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_olaparib` had an adjusted coefficient of -0.026 months (SE about 0.017, p=0.1202), not significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` had an adjusted coefficient of 0.033 months (SE about 0.017, p=0.0502), borderline but not significant at 0.05.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_pembrolizumab` had an adjusted coefficient of -0.019 months (SE about 0.014, p=0.1696), not supporting a main-effect benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0.
Each 1-point higher `ecog_ps` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
Patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without brain metastases.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `stage_iv=1` was associated with -1.549 months of adjusted `pfs_months` (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1-point higher `ecog_ps` was associated with -1.172 months of adjusted `pfs_months` (p<1e-300).

analyses.result_summary: `has_brain_mets=1` was associated with -0.983 months of adjusted `pfs_months` (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `liver_mets` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without liver metastases.
Patients with `bone_mets` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without bone metastases.
Patients with `node_positive` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without node-positive disease.

analyses.result_summary: `liver_mets=1` had a small negative coefficient of -0.018 months, but this was not significant (p=0.1944).

analyses.result_summary: `bone_mets=1` had a small positive coefficient of 0.014 months, not significant (p=0.2732).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
Each 100 U/L higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
Each 5 percentage-point higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Each 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` was associated with 0.476 months higher adjusted `pfs_months` (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each 10 mg/L higher `crp_mg_l` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
Each 1-unit higher `nlr` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
Each 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
Each 1-SD larger `tumor_size_cm` is associated with lower adjusted mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Each 10 mg/L higher `crp_mg_l` had a near-zero coefficient of -0.001 months (p=0.8348).

analyses.result_summary: Each 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct` was associated with -0.189 months of adjusted `pfs_months` (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: Larger `tumor_size_cm` had a near-zero positive adjusted coefficient of 0.002 months per SD (p=0.6496), refuting the negative hypothesis.

Patients with `pr_positive` equal to 1 have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `pr_positive` equal to 0.
Patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1 have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0.
Patients with `her2_low` equal to 1 have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_low` equal to 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is larger in `er_positive` patients than in `er_positive`-negative patients.
The positive association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is larger in `postmenopausal` patients than in non-postmenopausal patients.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_tamoxifen * er_positive interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_tamoxifen` by `er_positive` interaction was 0.030 months and was not significant (p=0.2105).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_tamoxifen * postmenopausal interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_tamoxifen` by `postmenopausal` interaction was -0.003 months and was not significant (p=0.8957).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger in `er_positive` patients than in `er_positive`-negative patients.
The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger in `pr_positive` patients than in `pr_positive`-negative patients.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_palbociclib * er_positive interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_palbociclib` by `er_positive` interaction was strongly positive: 1.559 months (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_palbociclib * pr_positive interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_palbociclib` by `pr_positive` interaction was strongly positive: 1.029 months (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `pik3ca_mutation` than in patients without `pik3ca_mutation`.
The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in `her2_positive` patients than in `her2_positive`-negative patients.
The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger in `her2_low` patients than in patients with `her2_low` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_palbociclib * pik3ca_mutation interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_palbociclib` by `pik3ca_mutation` interaction was -1.665 months (p<1e-300), indicating marked attenuation in PIK3CA-mutated disease.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_palbociclib * her2_positive interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_palbociclib` by `her2_positive` interaction was -1.337 months (p<1e-300), indicating weaker association in HER2-positive disease.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_palbociclib * her2_low interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_palbociclib` by `her2_low` interaction was 0.382 months (p<1e-300), supporting a larger association in HER2-low disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `er_positive=1`, `her2_positive=0`, and `pik3ca_mutation=0`, `treatment_palbociclib=1` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than no palbociclib.
Within patients with `er_positive=1` and `her2_positive=0`, the positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller when `pik3ca_mutation=1`.

analyses.code: Adjusted subgroup OLS among er_positive=1, her2_positive=0, pik3ca_mutation=0.
analyses.result_summary: In the ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype subgroup, `treatment_palbociclib=1` was associated with 2.920 months higher adjusted `pfs_months` (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS in er_positive=1 and her2_positive=0 subgroup with treatment_palbociclib * pik3ca_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: Within ER-positive/HER2-negative patients, the palbociclib by PIK3CA interaction was -2.918 months (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is larger in `her2_positive` patients than in `her2_positive`-negative patients.
The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `her2_amplification` than in patients without `her2_amplification`.
The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is weaker in patients with `her2_low` than in patients with `her2_low` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_trastuzumab * her2_positive interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_trastuzumab` by `her2_positive` interaction was 0.022 months, not significant (p=0.4951).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_trastuzumab * her2_amplification interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_trastuzumab` by `her2_amplification` interaction was -0.052 months, not significant (p=0.4866).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_trastuzumab * her2_low interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_trastuzumab` by `her2_low` interaction was -0.012 months, not significant (p=0.6540).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with either `brca1_mutation=1` or `brca2_mutation=1` than in patients without either mutation.
The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `brca1_mutation` than in patients without `brca1_mutation`.
The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `brca2_mutation` than in patients without `brca2_mutation`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_olaparib * any BRCA1/2 mutation interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_olaparib` by any-BRCA interaction was 0.018 months, not significant (p=0.8241).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_olaparib * brca1_mutation interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_olaparib` by `brca1_mutation` interaction was 0.079 months, not significant (p=0.4768).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_olaparib * brca2_mutation interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_olaparib` by `brca2_mutation` interaction was -0.045 months, not significant (p=0.6859).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive=0`, `pr_positive=0`, and `her2_positive=0`) than in other patients.

The positive association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with visceral metastases (`liver_mets`, `adrenal_mets`, `contralateral_lung_mets`, or `pleural_effusion`) than in patients without visceral metastases.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan * triple_negative interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` by triple-negative interaction was 0.047 months, not significant (p=0.2497).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan * visceral_mets interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` by visceral-metastases interaction was 0.020 months, not significant (p=0.5592).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is larger when `has_brain_mets=1` than when `has_brain_mets=0`.
The association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is more negative among `stage_iv` patients than among non-`stage_iv` patients.
The association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is more negative among patients with `liver_mets` than among patients without `liver_mets`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan * has_brain_mets interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` by `has_brain_mets` interaction was 0.193 months and significant (p=0.0021), a small positive subgroup signal.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan * stage_iv interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted sacituzumab by `stage_iv` interaction was 0.034 months, not significant (p=0.3623), opposite the hypothesized negative direction.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan * liver_mets interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted sacituzumab by `liver_mets` interaction was 0.014 months, not significant (p=0.7696), opposite the hypothesized negative direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive=0`, `pr_positive=0`, and `her2_positive=0`) than in other patients.
The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `crp_mg_l >= 10` than in patients with lower `crp_mg_l`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_pembrolizumab * triple_negative interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_pembrolizumab` by triple-negative interaction was 0.032 months, not significant (p=0.3550).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_pembrolizumab * high_crp interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_pembrolizumab` by high-CRP interaction was 0.042 months, not significant (p=0.2782), not supporting reduced benefit with high CRP.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `autoimmune_disease` than in patients without `autoimmune_disease`.
The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` decreases as `nlr` increases.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_pembrolizumab * autoimmune_disease interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `autoimmune_disease` interaction was 0.021 months, not significant (p=0.7492).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_pembrolizumab * standardized nlr interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `nlr` interaction was -0.014 months per SD of NLR, not significant (p=0.3316).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients who are both `stage_iv=1` and `liver_mets=1` have lower `pfs_months` than expected from additive `stage_iv` and `liver_mets` effects alone.
Patients who have both `has_brain_mets=1` and `pleural_effusion=1` have lower `pfs_months` than expected from additive effects alone.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with stage_iv * liver_mets interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `stage_iv` by `liver_mets` interaction was 0.044 months and not significant (p=0.1514), not supporting synergistic worsening.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with has_brain_mets * pleural_effusion interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `has_brain_mets` by `pleural_effusion` interaction was -0.061 months and not significant (p=0.1954).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` and `pfs_months` is stronger in patients with `albumin_g_dl < 3.5` than in patients with higher albumin.
The negative association between high proliferation (`ki67_pct >= 30`) and `pfs_months` is weaker in `er_positive` patients than in `er_positive`-negative patients.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with low_albumin * weight_loss_pct_6mo_per_5 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The low-albumin by weight-loss interaction was -0.025 months per 5 percentage points of weight loss, not significant (p=0.1078).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with high_ki67 * er_positive interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The high-Ki67 by ER-positive interaction was -0.598 months and significant (p<1e-300), opposite the hypothesized weaker adverse effect in ER-positive disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both `er_positive=1` and `pr_positive=1` have higher `pfs_months` than expected from additive ER and PR effects alone.
`her2_positive` patients have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than `her2_low` patients, comparing these HER2-defined subgroups after adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with er_positive * pr_positive interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `er_positive` by `pr_positive` interaction was 0.023 months, not significant (p=0.4673).

analyses.code: Adjusted linear contrast: coefficient(her2_positive) - coefficient(her2_low).
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted contrast for `her2_positive` minus `her2_low` was -0.473 months (p<1e-300), refuting the hypothesis of higher PFS in HER2-positive patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each 1-year higher `age_years` is associated with higher adjusted mean `pfs_months`.
Patients with `chronic_kidney_disease=1` have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without chronic kidney disease.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: Each additional year of `age_years` was associated with 0.176 months higher adjusted `pfs_months` (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `chronic_kidney_disease=1` had a small coefficient of -0.013 months and was not significant (p=0.4211).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `diabetes_mellitus=1` have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without diabetes mellitus.
Patients with `hypertension=1` have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without hypertension.
Patients with `heart_failure=1` have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without heart failure.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `diabetes_mellitus=1` had a negligible positive coefficient of 0.002 months, not significant (p=0.8672).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `hypertension=1` had a near-zero coefficient of 0.000 months, not significant (p=0.9742).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `heart_failure=1` had a small positive coefficient of 0.007 months, not significant (p=0.6629).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is larger when either `her2_positive=1` or `her2_amplification=1` than otherwise.
The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is larger in any BRCA-mutated disease than in BRCA-wildtype disease when BRCA1 and BRCA2 are combined into one subgroup.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_trastuzumab * (her2_positive or her2_amplification) interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Using a combined HER2-positive-or-amplified subgroup, the trastuzumab interaction was -0.007 months and not significant (p=0.8461).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_olaparib * any BRCA1/2 mutation interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Using a combined BRCA1/2-mutated subgroup, the olaparib interaction was 0.018 months and not significant (p=0.8241).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `copd=1` have lower adjusted mean `pfs_months` than patients without COPD.

The largest routine comorbidity coefficients (`diabetes_mellitus`, `hypertension`, `copd`, `chronic_kidney_disease`, and `heart_failure`) are much smaller in magnitude than the `stage_iv`, `ecog_ps`, and palbociclib interaction effects.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for pfs_months with demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and all treatment indicators as covariates.
analyses.result_summary: `copd=1` had a near-zero coefficient of -0.001 months and was not significant (p=0.9073).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS comparing routine comorbidity coefficients to major oncology predictors.
analyses.result_summary: The largest absolute coefficient among the five routine comorbidity indicators was about 0.013 months, with no significant comorbidity signal; this was far smaller than stage IV (-1.549), ECOG (-1.172 per point), and palbociclib subgroup effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The dominant clinically meaningful treatment pattern is that `treatment_palbociclib` is associated with higher `pfs_months` primarily in ER-positive and PR-positive disease and is attenuated by `pik3ca_mutation` and `her2_positive` status.
After multivariable adjustment, disease burden and tumor biology variables have stronger associations with `pfs_months` than routine inflammatory markers `crp_mg_l` and `nlr`.

analyses.code: Synthesis across palbociclib interaction and subgroup OLS models.
analyses.result_summary: The synthesis of interaction tests supported this pattern: palbociclib interactions were +1.559 months with ER positivity, +1.029 with PR positivity, -1.665 with PIK3CA mutation, -1.337 with HER2 positivity, and +2.920 in ER-positive/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-wildtype subgroup analysis; all were p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Synthesis across adjusted main-effect OLS models.
analyses.result_summary: CRP (-0.001 months per 10 mg/L, p=0.8348) and NLR (-0.001 months per unit, p=0.5445) were null, while stage IV (-1.549), ECOG (-1.172), brain metastases (-0.983), PIK3CA mutation (-0.582), and Ki-67 (-0.189 per 10 points) were significant adverse predictors.
